name: Smart Capture Commit
description: Commits a single capture intent (after the user has confirmed). Pair with `capture` — call `capture` first to extract, present results to the user, then call this skill once per intent the user accepted. The `payload` shape depends on the domain. Domains and their payload fields: tool ({name, brand, model, type, serial, condition, notes, location}), router_bit ({brand, profile, shank_size_in, cutting_diameter_in, cutting_length_in, has_bearing, set_name, notes}), saw_blade ({brand, diameter_in, teeth, kerf_in, type, notes}), wood ({species, dimensions, quantity, unit, notes}), album ({title, artist, label, catalog_number, year, condition, notes}), manual ({title, brand, model, doc_type, notes}), house_room ({name, floor, notes}), recipe ({title, source, servings, ingredients (string array), method (string array), notes}), cook_log ({recipe_query, cooked_at, servings_made, adjustments, rating, notes}), shopping_list ({items (string array)}), knowledge_fact ({topic, fact}), project ({title, type, goals, budget, notes}), other ({suggested_title, content (markdown body)}). If an asset_id was returned by the extract step (image was uploaded), pass it through here so the file gets linked to the new record.
author: Michael

domain.description: Required. One of: tool, saw_blade, wood, album, manual, house_room, recipe, cook_log, shopping_list, knowledge_fact, project, other.

payload.description: Required. Object whose shape depends on the domain (see skill description). Pass only the fields that are actually known; missing fields are accepted as null.

asset_id.description: Optional. UUID of a files.assets row returned by the extract step. When provided, the new record is linked to the asset (e.g., the photo of the tool gets attached to the inventory.tools row).

extract_token.description: Required. The extract_token returned by the `capture` skill's response. This proves you received a successful extract. Commits without a valid token are rejected.
